extraction.one_line_summary: KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren won the 15km technical ITT at 48km/h average, taking 12 seconds from Asgreen and making GC leader SCHACHMANN second.
tactical_patterns: Kragh Andersen paced negative split perfectly after intermediate checkpoint, gaining time in second half unlike Bilbao who faded.
Schachmann rode defensively, only 7sec up at checkpoint but losing 5sec to winner—prioritized GC gaps over stage hunting.
Alaphilippe's home-course advantage worthless on technical TT despite climbing fast past rivals, finished 24 seconds down in 11th.

rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSEN Søren: positive: first pro TT win at 48km/h reveals hidden engine, pacing discipline superior to established specialists like Asgreen
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Max: positive: extended GC lead to 58 seconds despite losing stage, smart conservative ride preserved energy over rivals
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: neutral: strong first half (3sec up on De Gent) but couldn't sustain power, suggests insufficient ITT-specific training
rider_signals.ASGREEN Kasper: negative: won Kuurne earlier but only third here 13sec back, suggests single-day specialist form over stage race endurance
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: home circuit advantage nullified, 24sec deficit suggests technical descending skill doesn't compensate for raw power deficit
rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: positive: only 46sec down as young climber type, better than established ITT riders Pinot/Nibali shows developing diesel engine
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: new Lapierre TT bike irrelevant at 50sec down, confirms chronic weakness in discipline even on medium-length efforts
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: 53sec loss as team GC leader at Trek reveals declining form, worse than pure climber Higuita
